text: The surgeon utilizes the TilePro input port on the DaVinci XI robotic system to access and display the three-dimensional modeling of the surgical area during the procedure.
The patient is positioned in a modified lithotomy position, and ureteric stents are visibly inserted.
Under visual guidance, four robotic ports and one assistant port are inserted into the patient's abdomen, facilitating access for the robotic surgical instruments.
The surgeon encounters dense adhesions in the small bowel from the patient's previous anterior resection and proceeds to divide them. Subsequently, all bowel loops are reflected superiorly.
The surgeon dissects the parietal peritoneum over the right common iliac artery, exposing the underlying anatomical structures.
The surgeon identifies and preserves the left common iliac artery and both ureters, ensuring these structures remain intact during the procedure.
The surgeon continues dissection past the aortic bifurcation using a monopolar energy device, focusing on precise tissue separation in the surgical field.
The surgeon identifies a malignant node at the aortic bifurcation and performs sharp dissection to remove it, utilizing live intraoperative referencing to a 3D reconstruction for precise navigation.
The surgeon uses the three-dimensional reconstruction to establish a dissection plane between the malignant node and the anterior aortic wall, navigating through dense fibrotic tissue.
The surgeon places the specimen into an endo-catch bag for removal from the surgical field.
